# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

We want CodeLedger to be a welcoming, safe, and constructive space for everyone.

Participants are expected to:
- Be respectful and professional.
- Assume good intent and discuss ideas, not people.
- Give actionable feedback.
- Help keep discussions focused on improving the project.

## Unacceptable Behavior

The following behavior is not acceptable:
- Harassment, hate speech, discrimination, or personal attacks.
- Threats, intimidation, or doxxing.
- Sexualized language or unwelcome sexual attention.
- Spam, trolling, or intentionally disruptive behavior.
- Sharing private information without consent.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies in all project spaces, including:
- GitHub issues, pull requests, and discussions.
- Community chats and project-linked communication channels.

## Enforcement

Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing this policy.

They may take actions they consider appropriate, including:
- Requesting behavior changes.
- Removing content.
- Temporarily or permanently restricting participation.

## Reporting

If you experience or witness behavior that violates this Code of Conduct, report it privately to the maintainers.

When possible, include:
- Links/screenshots.
- Timestamps.
- A short summary of what happened.

All reports will be reviewed promptly and handled with care.

## Attribution

This policy is inspired by common open source community standards and adapted for CodeLedger.
